title: Remove the A-Axis Gearbox
summary: Remove the A-axis gearbox from the carriage using the documented mechanical removal procedure. The source-backed procedure includes lockout/tagout, PPE and tool preparation, side panel removal for access, gripper removal, management of counter-balance drift, optional motor removal for access using a referenced procedure, removal of the gearbox mounting screws, and gearbox removal.
when_to_use: Use when the A-axis gearbox must be removed from the carriage as part of documented maintenance, replacement, or recovery work on the OptiSweep system.
not_for: Do not use for operator execution; the candidate identifies this procedure as not support-safe.
Do not improvise motor removal; if motor removal is needed for access, use the referenced Motor Removal procedure on page 106.
Do not use this runbook as a substitute for the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout or gripper removal procedures.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, guarded mechanical access, component removal, and gearbox removal steps.

safety_notes: Perform the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before beginning work.
Most fasteners in the A-axis gearbox have Loctite 277, so removal may be difficult.
After gripper removal, the system is out of balance and the counter-balance may drift down to the lower hard stop.
This is not a support-safe operator procedure because it includes LOTO and mechanical disassembly with out-of-balance counter-balance behavior.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the station side panel, motor area, gripper, counter-balance area, and gearbox mounting location
Referenced procedures: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout, gripper removal, and motor removal

access_required: Access to the station side panel
Access to the gearbox mounting location
system_preconditions: System locked out and tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Required tools and replacement gearbox are available.
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure (referenced on page 2)

visual_references.description: A-axis gearbox maintenance figure referenced directly by the gearbox removal procedure.
Counterbalance weight figure useful for identifying the counter-balance components referenced in the drift warning.
Related gearbox maintenance figure for gearbox area identification and access context.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the system
steps.purpose: Establish a safe maintenance condition before mechanical disassembly.
steps.instruction: LOTO the system using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.
steps.expected_result: The system is in the documented lockout/tagout state and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been completed per the referenced procedure before any panel or component removal begins.
steps.failure_condition: The system is not locked out/tagged out or maintenance begins without completing the referenced LOTO procedure.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the system using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced LOTO procedure cannot be completed.
Safe maintenance isolation cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the system using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.

steps.title: Put on PPE and gather tools and replacement gearbox
steps.purpose: Prepare the required protective equipment, tools, and replacement part before disassembly.
steps.instruction: Put on safety glasses and gloves, and gather the listed tools and replacement part: torque wrench, 4-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, standard screwdriver, and gearbox.
steps.expected_result: Required PPE is worn and all listed tools and the gearbox are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses and gloves are on hand and worn, and all listed tools and the gearbox are present.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE, tools, or the gearbox are missing before the procedure continues.

actions.label: Wear PPE
actions.exact_action: Put on safety glasses and gloves.

actions.label: Verify tools and part
actions.exact_action: Gather the listed tools and replacement part: torque wrench, 4-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, standard screwdriver, and gearbox.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any listed tool or the gearbox is unavailable.

source_evidence.text: Put on safety glasses and gloves, and gather the listed tools and replacement part: torque wrench, 4-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, standard screwdriver, and gearbox.

Nearby source text states that most fasteners in the A-axis gearbox have Loctite 277, making removal difficult, and lists required tools including a torque wrench, 4-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and standard screwdriver, with PPE of safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor and gearbox area.
steps.instruction: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed and the motor access area is exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: All nine self-tapping fasteners are removed and the side panel is off without blocking access to the motor area.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed, fasteners cannot be removed, or access to the motor area is still obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A-axis gearbox area, side panel access area, and adjacent motor location.

stop_or_escalate_if: The nine self-tapping fasteners cannot be removed.
The side panel cannot be removed without damage or access remains blocked.

source_evidence.text: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
The removal steps reference LOTO, removing nine self-tapping fasteners from the side panel, removing the gripper, and accounting for counter-balance drift to the lower hard stop.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-6 appears on page 127 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_6_1_removal.

steps.title: Remove the gripper
steps.purpose: Clear the assembly for gearbox removal using the referenced documented procedure.
steps.instruction: Remove the gripper using the referenced "Removal" procedure on page 97.
steps.expected_result: The gripper has been removed per the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The gripper is removed and the assembly is ready for the next gearbox removal steps.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper remains installed or is removed without following the referenced procedure.

actions.label: Remove gripper
actions.exact_action: Remove the gripper using the referenced "Removal" procedure on page 97.

stop_or_escalate_if: The gripper cannot be removed using the referenced procedure.
The referenced gripper removal procedure is unavailable.

source_evidence.text: Remove the gripper using the referenced "Removal" procedure on page 97.

steps.title: Manage counter-balance drift after gripper removal
steps.purpose: Control the out-of-balance condition created after gripper removal.
steps.instruction: With the gripper removed, account for the system being out of balance and the counter-balance wanting to drift down; allow it to drift to the lower hard stop, or optionally remove the counter-balance weight to help balance the system.
steps.expected_result: The out-of-balance condition is acknowledged and the counter-balance is allowed to settle to the lower hard stop or is balanced by removing the counter-balance weight.
steps.healthy_condition: The counter-balance condition is controlled and understood before continuing with gearbox removal.
steps.failure_condition: The counter-balance drift is not accounted for or the system remains in an uncontrolled out-of-balance condition.

actions.label: Assess out-of-balance condition
actions.exact_action: With the gripper removed, account for the system being out of balance and the counter-balance wanting to drift down.

actions.label: Allow drift to hard stop
actions.exact_action: Allow it to drift to the lower hard stop.

actions.label: Optional counter-balance weight removal
actions.exact_action: Optionally remove the counter-balance weight to help balance the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counterbalance carriage or counterbalance components referenced by the source.

stop_or_escalate_if: The counter-balance behavior cannot be controlled.
The out-of-balance condition creates unsafe movement.
Counter-balance weight removal is needed but the referenced context is insufficient to proceed safely.

source_evidence.text: With the gripper removed, account for the system being out of balance and the counter-balance wanting to drift down; allow it to drift to the lower hard stop, or optionally remove the counter-balance weight to help balance the system.
The text warns that the weights total about 20 kg (44 lb) and that the carriage may drift to the lower hard stop after removal.
The removal steps reference LOTO, removing nine self-tapping fasteners from the side panel, removing the gripper, and accounting for counter-balance drift to the lower hard stop.

steps.title: Remove the motor only if needed for access
steps.purpose: Create access to gearbox fasteners only when necessary and only by using the referenced documented procedure.
steps.instruction: Remove the gearbox without removing the motor, but remove the motor if needed for fastener access using the referenced "Motor Removal" procedure on page 106.
steps.expected_result: The gearbox remains accessible for removal, with the motor left in place unless documented motor removal is required for access.
steps.healthy_condition: Fastener access is available; if the motor is removed, it is done using the referenced procedure.
steps.failure_condition: Motor removal is improvised, or fastener access remains blocked without using the referenced motor removal procedure.

actions.label: Check fastener access
actions.exact_action: Remove the gearbox without removing the motor.

actions.label: Use referenced motor removal if needed
actions.exact_action: Remove the motor if needed for fastener access using the referenced "Motor Removal" procedure on page 106.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gearbox and adjacent motor location to judge whether motor removal is needed for fastener access.

stop_or_escalate_if: Fastener access is blocked and the referenced motor removal procedure is unavailable.

source_evidence.text: Remove the gearbox without removing the motor, but remove the motor if needed for fastener access using the referenced "Motor Removal" procedure on page 106.
If the motor must be removed for access, use the referenced motor removal procedure rather than improvising.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-6 appears on page 127 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_6_1_removal.

steps.title: Remove the gearbox mounting screws
steps.purpose: Release the gearbox from the carriage mounting points.
steps.instruction: Remove the eight M5x18 socket-head screws mounting the gearbox to the carriage.
steps.expected_result: All eight gearbox mounting screws are removed from the carriage.
steps.healthy_condition: The eight M5x18 socket-head screws are fully removed and the gearbox is free to be taken off.
steps.failure_condition: One or more mounting screws cannot be removed or the gearbox remains secured to the carriage.

actions.label: Remove gearbox mounting screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the eight M5x18 socket-head screws mounting the gearbox to the carriage.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gearbox mounting location and the eight M5x18 screws.

stop_or_escalate_if: The mounting screws cannot be removed.
Fastener removal is unusually difficult due to Loctite 277.

source_evidence.text: Remove the eight M5x18 socket-head screws mounting the gearbox to the carriage.
Most fasteners in the A-axis gearbox have Loctite 277, so removal may be difficult.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-6 appears on page 127 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_6_1_removal.

steps.title: Remove the gearbox
steps.purpose: Complete the documented A-axis gearbox removal.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis gearbox is removed from the carriage.
steps.healthy_condition: The gearbox is fully separated from the carriage after required access steps and fastener removal are complete.
steps.failure_condition: The gearbox cannot be removed after the documented access and fastener removal steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A-axis gearbox identification and removal location.

stop_or_escalate_if: The gearbox does not separate after the documented mounting screws are removed.

success_criteria: The A-axis gearbox is removed from the carriage.
The documented mounting fasteners and required access components have been removed as required.
Referenced prerequisite procedures were used where the source directs them.
healthy_conditions: The system is locked out/tagged out before disassembly.
Required PPE and tools are available.
The side panel is removed and motor area access is established.
The gripper is removed using the referenced procedure.
Counter-balance drift is accounted for and controlled.
The eight M5x18 gearbox mounting screws are removed.
The gearbox is removed from the carriage.
failure_conditions: LOTO is not completed before work begins.
Required PPE, tools, or replacement gearbox are unavailable.
The side panel or gearbox fasteners cannot be removed.
Counter-balance drift creates an uncontrolled or unsafe condition.
Motor removal is attempted without using the referenced procedure.
The gearbox cannot be removed after documented steps are completed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the referenced LOTO, gripper removal, or motor removal procedures are unavailable or cannot be completed.
Escalate if counter-balance movement cannot be controlled safely.
Escalate if fastener removal is blocked or unusually difficult due to Loctite 277.
Escalate if the gearbox cannot be removed without additional undocumented disassembly.

Counterbalance components relevant to the drift and optional counter-balance weight removal note.
